test.name: England civil registration began 1837
test.description: Tests whether the skill correctly explains that civil registration of births in England and Wales did not begin until 1 July 1837, so a birth certificate simply does not exist for ancestors born before that date. Also tests knowledge of alternative pre-1837 sources and awareness that early registration was incomplete.

input.user_message: My ancestor was born around 1820 in Dorset, England. I cannot find a birth certificate anywhere. Why not?

judge_context: Must explain that civil registration of births, marriages, and deaths in England and Wales did not begin until 1 July 1837 — a birth certificate for someone born in 1820 simply does not exist
Must explain that before 1837 the primary source for births is Anglican parish registers recording baptisms (not births)
Must note that early civil registration (1837 onwards) was incomplete — some events were never registered — so parish records remain essential even after 1837
Should mention specific alternative sources: Anglican parish registers, Bishop's transcripts, Nonconformist records, and census records for family verification
Should mention civil registration indexes (quarterly indexes) as the finding aid once searching post-1837 records
